entities.name: LIGHT FRACTION CARBON
entities.description: A physically distinct and relatively unstable component of soil organic matter consisting of partially decomposed residues and serving as an indicator of soil health.

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of soil that contains light fraction carbon.

entities.name: PARTIALLY DECOMPOSED RESIDUES
entities.description: The material that constitutes light fraction carbon.

entities.name: MANAGEMENT IMPACTS
entities.description: The effects of agricultural or land management practices on soil.

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The state of soil functionality and quality, indicated by light fraction carbon.

entities.name: DENSITY FRACTIONATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Techniques used to isolate light fraction carbon from soil.

entities.name: LABILE CARBON POOL
entities.description: The pool of easily decomposable carbon providing insights into nutrient cycling and sustainability.

entities.name: NUTRIENT CYCLING
entities.description: The process of movement and exchange of organic and inorganic nutrients.

entities.name: SOIL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to maintain productivity and function over time.
